Sections marked as panels expand to show their items; Flows opens straight to its own page. # The Make Content button The Make Content button in the global header is the fastest way to start something new, from anywhere in the app. It opens a menu with: Upload Episode , to upload a video or audio file as a new episode. Import Episodes , to import existing content, such as from YouTube. Upload Co

title: Set up your brand with the onboarding wizard
description: The five-step wizard that captures your brand voice, audience, and visual style so everything Vidflow generates sounds and looks like you.
body: The onboarding wizard is the first thing a new workspace sees. It captures your brand name, audience, voice, and visual style in five short steps, then feeds every one of those answers straight into the AI tools you use later, so your first clips, posts, and images already sound like you instead of a generic assistant. # How it works Every workspace has an onboardedAt flag that starts empty. Until it is set, Vidflow redirects you into the wizard the moment you land on any app page. Finishing the wizard, or skipping it, sets that flag and the redirect stops firing. Everything you enter is saved directly to your workspace's Brand Settings: brand basics, target audience, brand voice guidelines, your voice profile, and your image style prompt, so nothing here is a separate copy that later needs merging in. # The five steps Welcome. A fork in the road: Fill it out myself walks you through the remaining steps by hand. Use my AI to fill this out is where the AI Shortcut lives, copy a ready-made prompt into ChatGPT, Claude, or another assistant, paste its answer back, and Vidflow parses it straight into your brand profile. See Get the most out of Vidflow by looping in your AI for the full walkthrough of that shortcut, including where to find it if it isn't showing on this step yet. Who you are. Your brand name, an optional website, and an optional industry. If you have a YouTube channel, paste its URL or @handle into Import from YouTube and click Import , Vidflow pulls your logo, bra

title: Quickstart: one video to a week of content
description: The fastest path from your first sign-in to a week of scheduled content, one step at a time.
headings: What you have at the end
body: This is the golden path through Vidflow: from your first sign-in to a full week of content sitting on your calendar. Follow it once, in order, and every later visit is faster. # How it works A new workspace walks you through most of this automatically. Signing in for the first time drops you into a short onboarding wizard that captures your brand voice and look, then a first-episode screen that takes a YouTube link or an uploaded file and builds a starter content pack from it, transcribed, written in your voice, and queued to your calendar, without you touching the Clip Generator or Magic Text Posts by hand. The steps below are the same underlying path spelled out in full, useful both for that first run and for every video after it. Note Note: New workspaces are walked through steps 3 and 4 below automatically by the onboarding wizard and the first-episode screen. See Set up your brand with the onboarding wizard and Your first content pack . This page still holds if you skipped either one or you're adding your second episode onward. # The path Get invited and sign in. Vidflow is invite-only during the beta. Once you have access, sign in with Google or with an email and password. See Getting access to Vidflow and Signing in and resetting your password . Confirm your workspace. Your first sign-in lands you on the Brand Spaces screen, and your first workspace is included on every plan. If you are setting up for a single brand, you likely already have what you need; agencies crea

title: Signing in and resetting your password
description: How to sign in with Google or email and password, and how to reset a forgotten password.
headings: Sign in with email and password
body: Vidflow supports two sign-in methods, and a self-serve password reset if you forget yours. # How it works Sign-in is checked against your invite before anything else, whichever method you use. If two-factor authentication is on for your account, it's an extra step after your password or Google login is accepted, not a replacement for either. A password reset always confirms it went through, whether or not that email actually has a Vidflow account, so the confirmation message itself never leaks whether an email is registered. # Sign in with Google Go to app.vidflow.io. Click Continue with Google . Choose the Google account tied to your Vidflow invite. Your email address must be verified with Google and must match an email that has been granted Vidflow access. See Getting access to Vidflow if you have not been invited yet. # Sign in with email and password Go to app.vidflow.io. Enter your email and password. Click Sign in . If your account has two-factor authentication turned on, you are prompted for a second step after your password is accepted. See Account security for how to set up and manage two-factor authentication. # Resetting your password On the sign-in screen, click Forgot password? . Enter the email address you sign in with and submit the form. Check your inbox for an email with the subject "Reset your Vidflow password." For your privacy, this step always confirms the request went through, whether or not that email has a Vidflow account. Click the reset link in the e

title: What is Vidflow?
description: How Vidflow turns one long-form video into a month of on-brand content, and who it is built for.
body: Vidflow turns one long-form video into clips, captions, and text posts in your brand voice, then schedules everything across your channels, with client approval from one shareable link. # The core idea You upload or import a single long-form video or audio file. Vidflow calls that source an episode. From that one episode, Vidflow can generate short clips, quote and insight posts, blog articles, and images, all written to sound like your brand rather than a generic AI voice. From there you schedule the pieces across your connected accounts or send them to a client for approval first. The goal is one upload in, a full week (or month) of content out. # How it works Every episode gets transcribed first, since almost everything downstream reads that transcript. From there, an AI reads the transcript to pick the strongest moments for clips, write quote and insight posts, and draft captions, all shaped by the brand voice and target audience you set up once in your workspace. New workspaces walk through a short onboarding wizard that captures that brand voice up front, then your first episode automatically generates a starter batch of content so you see the whole loop before you touch a single settings screen. # Who it is for Vidflow is built for video-first creators, podcasters, coaches, and personal brands who record long-form content and need it turned into a steady stream of shorter posts. It also works for agencies running content for multiple client brands at once, since each b

title: The Calendar
description: View, schedule, and manage your posts across a month, week, day, or list view.
headings: Facebook and Instagram: know which kind of post you have
body: The Calendar is where you see everything scheduled to publish, drag content onto a date and time, and manage or fix individual posts. # How it works The Calendar pulls from two places at once: content items scheduled through your schedules and queues, and standalone posts created directly as calendar entries. It merges both into one view, but they aren't dispatched by the same code path, which is why one kind of post (library content on a schedule) can auto-publish to Facebook and Instagram while the other (older calendar queue posts) cannot, see below. Dragging or tapping a slot just sets a date and time; the actual publish happens later, when the scheduled poller reaches that time. # Views Open the Calendar and switch views with the switcher at the top: month , week , day , or list . A Today button jumps back to the current date from anywhere. Month view shows up to 3 posts per day cell, with a "+{n} more" link when a day has more. Week and day views show a time grid from 6 AM to 11 PM in 30-minute slots, with a red line marking the current time on today's column. List view groups posts by day (for example "Today · {Month} {Day}") and shows a red Failed badge on any post with a failed status. An empty day reads "Nothing scheduled yet." # Scheduling content Drag and drop Drag a post from the Unscheduled rail onto a day. Holding it over a day for about half a second opens a Select time popover with hour buttons (for example "9 AM") to pick the exact time. Tap to schedule On t

title: Post now
description: Publish a piece of content immediately to a connected platform, without scheduling it.
headings: Post a piece of content immediately
body: Post now sends a single item straight to a connected account, skipping the calendar and queues entirely. # How it works Post now talks directly to each platform's own publishing API using your stored connection, one platform at a time, right when you click send. It bypasses approval gating and any tag-based queue logic since there's no schedule involved, so it publishes exactly what you see in the dialog the moment you confirm it. For TikTok specifically, Vidflow queries your account's live posting rules (who can view, comments, duet, stitch) from TikTok itself every time the dialog opens, so the options you're offered always reflect your current TikTok settings, not a cached copy. # Where to find it Post now is available from: The menu on any card in the Content Library . The Edit content modal for a post or clip. The content detail view, opened from a library card. # Post a piece of content immediately Open Post now from the card menu, the edit modal, or the detail view. Choose a platform from the pills shown. Pills only appear for platforms with a connected account: X, LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok. Review or edit the caption in the text box. A live character counter shows your length against that platform's limit. If TikTok is selected, fill in the TikTok settings panel (see below). Click Post now (or the platform-specific send action) to publish. A successful post shows "Posted to {label}!", with a link to view it when the platform returns one. A failed attem

title: Post statuses and failed posts
description: What each post status means, how failures are reported, and how to retry a failed post.
body: Every post in Vidflow carries a status that tells you where it is in the publishing process, plus a separate approval state if approvals apply to it. # How it works A background process checks every minute for content whose scheduled time has passed and picks it up for publishing. Each targeted platform is attempted independently, in its own step, so one platform failing doesn't stop the others from being tried. The final status reflects the outcome across all of them: any success marks the post Published, only an across-the-board failure marks it Failed, with the specific per-platform result kept either way so you can see exactly what happened on each one. # Post statuses Status Meaning Draft Not yet scheduled or sent anywhere. Queued Sitting in a queue schedule, waiting for its next open slot. Scheduled Placed on the calendar with a specific date and time. Publishing Actively being sent to one or more platforms right now. Published Successfully sent to at least one platform. Failed Every attempted platform failed. # Approval is a separate dimension A post also has an approval state, independent of the statuses above: needs approval, approved, or rejected. A schedule with Require Approval turned on will not dispatch an item until it reaches approved. See Client approvals overview and Approval status in your library . # What "failed" means Vidflow publishes to every platform an item is set to post on, and it evaluates each platform independently. If every platform attempt fai

title: Schedules and queues
description: Automate posting with recurring timeslots or an ordered queue, with optional tag filters and approval gating.
headings: Video-only platforms skip what they can't post
Recycle Content and Use Optimal Times
Editing, pausing, and deleting a schedule
body: A schedule automates where and when your content publishes, so you don't have to place every post on the calendar by hand. # How it works Every schedule is pinned to exactly one platform. A Timeslot schedule publishes at the recurring times you set. A Queue schedule instead holds an ordered list and releases one item at a time into its next open slot, whenever content is added to it, either automatically by a tag match or manually. When content is assigned to a queue, Vidflow checks whether that platform actually needs a video: TikTok and YouTube do, so a text or image item offered to one of those queues is left alone rather than being placed on the queue and failing later. It stays available for a schedule or platform that can actually take it. Facebook, Instagram, X, and LinkedIn queues accept any content type. # Create a schedule Open Schedules & Queues . Click Create Schedule . Enter a Schedule Name (for example "Daily Instagram Posts"). Select one or more Platforms . Choose a Type : Queue (ordered) or Timeslot (recurring) . Set up the posting schedule (see below) and any filters or toggles you want. Click Create Schedule . Every schedule targets exactly one platform under the hood. Picking more than one platform in step 4 doesn't create a single schedule that posts to all of them, it creates one schedule per platform you selected, each named "{Schedule Name} · {Platform}" and each with its own independent queue, using the same times, tags, and toggles you set up once. Ed

title: Publish episodes to YouTube and your podcast feed
description: Upload a full episode to YouTube and publish it to your workspace's podcast RSS feed.
body: Full episodes publish to YouTube, and to your podcast feed, from the episode itself rather than from Post now or the Calendar. # How it works An episode upload streams your video file directly to YouTube's resumable upload API, carrying over the title, description, and tags you've set on the episode, and lands at whatever privacy you chose. Your podcast feed is separate infrastructure entirely, a standard RSS feed Vidflow generates and hosts for your workspace, so publishing to YouTube and publishing to your feed are two independent actions on the same episode, not one step that does both. # Open the Publishing tab Open the episode from the Content Library . Go to its Publishing tab. You'll see two cards: YouTube and your podcast feed. # Upload to YouTube On the YouTube card, choose a privacy setting: Public , Private , or Unlisted . The default is Private . Start the upload. The episode's title, description, and tags feed the upload automatically. Once the upload completes, the card shows a Published badge. Because the default is Private, double-check the privacy dropdown before uploading if you want the video visible right away. # Publish to your podcast feed Your workspace has its own podcast RSS feed, independent of YouTube. On the podcast feed card: Publish to Feed adds the episode to your feed. Once added, the card reads Published to feed . If it hasn't been added yet, it reads Not in podcast feed . Your feed URL follows this pattern: /api/rss/your-workspace-slug It's a

title: Contact support
description: How to reach Vidflow support for product and billing issues, or privacy and data requests.
body: Use the right address for your issue, and include enough detail that support can act on your message right away. # How it works Vidflow support runs over email, routed by which address you send to: general product and billing questions go to one inbox, privacy and data-deletion requests go to a separate one so they get handled under the right process. There is no in-app chat widget or ticket portal today, so email is the primary channel. # Which address to use support@vidflow.io for product issues, publishing failures, billing questions, refunds, and account deletion. privacy@vidflow.io for privacy questions and data deletion requests. If you are requesting deletion of your data, use the subject line "Data Deletion Request" and send it from the email address on your account. See Your data and privacy . # What to include The more of this you include up front, the faster support can help: Your workspace name. Support needs to know which brand space the issue is on. The specific content item. Link to it in the Content Library, or include its title. What you expected to happen versus what actually happened. Describe both, not just the problem. Timestamps. When you took the action, and when the problem showed up. Screenshots. Especially of any error or failure text shown on screen. If a post failed, copy the exact failure text rather than paraphrasing it. See Fix publishing problems for where that text appears. Note Note: Vidflow does not publish a guaranteed response time. Includ

body: Quick answers to common questions, each with a link to the full article if you need more detail. # How do I get access to Vidflow? Vidflow is invite-only during the current beta. Join the waitlist from the marketing site, and you will get access in waves. Once you are approved, you receive an email titled "Your Vidflow access is ready" with a link to sign in at app.vidflow.io using that same email address. See Getting access to Vidflow . # What counts as an AI generation? AI generations are a shared monthly pool that includes transcribing an uploaded video, writing clip titles and captions, generating text posts, ideas, scripts, and images, and any other AI-written content. Transcribing a YouTube import does not draw from this pool the same way an upload does, since import quota is tracked separately from the AI pool. Check your plan's allowance and what happens at the cap in Usage limits and monthly allowances . # Why did my post fail? A post can fail for a few reasons: the connected account's authorization expired, the platform's requirements were not met (for example, no video on a platform that requires one, or a caption over the limit), or TikTok rejected the compose settings. The post's failure text tells you which platform failed and why. Work through Fix publishing problems in order, it is built as a checklist from most to least likely cause. # Why are my TikTok posts showing as private? Beta note Beta note: While TikTok's app review is pending, every post from Vidflo

title: Fix publishing problems
description: A checklist for failed posts, ordered by how likely each cause is, with the fix for each one.
headings: 1. Check if the account's authorization died
3. Check for a YouTube quota hold
4. Check for an old X connection missing media permission
5. Check if approval is holding it back
body: When a post fails, work through this checklist in order. It is ordered by how often each cause shows up, so start at the top. # How it works Every scheduled or queued post is picked up by a background dispatcher that publishes to each of its target platforms independently, records a per-platform result, and marks the item's overall status from those results. That is why one post can show as published while carrying a specific error for just one platform: each platform call succeeds or fails on its own. # 1. Check if the account's authorization died The most common cause of a failed post is a connected account whose authorization stopped working on the platform's end. When this happens, Vidflow pauses publishing to that account, emails the workspace, and marks it as needing reconnect. The email reads: "Your {platform} connection stopped working, so we've paused publishing to it. This usually happens when {platform} expires the authorization on their end, and it takes about a minute to fix. Reconnect the account and your scheduled posts will pick right back up." Fix it: Go to Workspace Settings > Connections . Find the account with a needs-reconnect status. Reconnect it by signing in again. Once reconnected, anything waiting on that account resumes normally. Note Note: Reconnect timing varies by platform. YouTube and TikTok refresh tokens automatically in the background and only need reconnect when the platform itself revokes access. Meta (Instagram and Facebook) tokens are lon

title: Get the most out of Vidflow by looping in your AI
description: Copy one prompt into ChatGPT or Claude, paste back the result, and watch your brand voice, audience, and image style fill in automatically.
headings: What each field means and how Vidflow uses it
If the paste does not parse
body: Setting up a brand voice from scratch means typing out how you sound, who you talk to, and what your content looks like. Most people already have an AI assistant that knows a lot of that about them already. The AI Shortcut lets that assistant do the typing. # Why loop in your AI If you already talk to ChatGPT, Claude, or another assistant about your business, it likely has a running picture of your brand voice, your audience, and what you post about. Instead of starting from a blank field, you hand it a prompt built for Vidflow, let it ask you a couple of quick questions to fill in any gaps, and bring the answer back. You can use the AI Shortcut in two places: on the welcome step of the onboarding wizard (click Use my AI to fill this out ), or any time later from this guide's steps below. Either way the flow is the same: copy the prompt, run it in your AI, paste the result back. # The one-paste shortcut Click Copy prompt . This copies a ready-made prompt written specifically for Vidflow's brand profile format. Paste the prompt into ChatGPT, Claude, or whichever AI assistant you use. Answer the handful of short questions it asks. If it already knows your business, it may only ask one or two, or skip straight to the answer. Copy the response your AI gives back, in full, including the code block. Paste it into the box on the Vidflow panel. As soon as you paste something, Vidflow reads it and shows a live preview: a checklist of every field it found, with the actual value underne

title: Set up your brand voice
description: Fill in your brand profile and voice settings so every clip title, caption, and post sounds like you.
body: Everything Vidflow generates, clip titles, captions, posts, scripts, draws on the brand voice settings in your workspace. Filling these in is the one setup step that changes how everything else sounds. Reach for this article any time generated content feels generic, before your first real batch of content, or whenever your brand's tone or audience shifts. # How it works Vidflow does not have a hardcoded writing style. Every AI generation call reads your workspace's brand fields (name, industry, target audience, brand sheet, voice guidelines, voice profile) and injects them into the prompt as context before it writes anything. An empty field is simply missing context, not a smarter default, so the AI falls back to a generic assistant voice. The more specific and current these fields are, the closer every clip title, caption, post, and script lands to how you actually sound. # Your Brand Profile Open Workspace Settings and go to the Brand tab. The Brand Profile card includes: Brand Name (required). Website , Industry , Location , and Timezone . Target Audience , a free-text field for describing your ideal listener: who they are, what they struggle with, what they want. Campaign Focus Areas , a set of topic chips you add yourself (for example, Leadership or Entrepreneurship) to steer what the AI treats as on-topic. Below that is the Brand Sheet card, where you paste or type a longer brand document, or upload one. This is the single biggest input into how on-brand your generated

title: Invite team members
description: Add teammates to a workspace by email and role, and how client reviewers work differently from working seats.
body: Bring teammates into a workspace so more than one person can work on a brand's content. Reach for this article when you are building out an agency team, giving a client review-only access, or figuring out why an invite did not go through. # How it works Every workspace member has exactly one role, checked on the server before any settings change, invite, or admin action: admin (full access), brand_manager (content and publishing access, no team or danger-zone access), or client (view and approve only). Sending an invite whitelists the email so it can sign in, writes a pending invite row, and emails the person a link. If they already have a Vidflow account under that email, they are added to the workspace immediately instead of waiting on the email. Note Note: Team access is based on seats. The workspace owner always counts as one seat, and each plan includes a set number of additional working seats (Admin + Brand Manager combined) per workspace: Free and Starter include 1 (the owner only), Pro includes 2, Agency includes 10 per brand space. Client reviewer seats are always free and unlimited. See Plans and features . # Send an invite Open Workspace Settings and go to the Team tab. In the Invite a teammate card, enter the person's email address. Choose a role: Admin , Brand Manager , or Client . Click Send invite . Sending an invite requires admin access on the workspace. If the invite email cannot be sent (for example email is not configured), Vidflow still creates the invite

title: Running multiple brands
description: How agencies run several client brands in Vidflow, one isolated workspace per brand.
body: Agencies running content for more than one client use a separate workspace for each brand. Reach for this article when you are onboarding a second client, deciding whether to upgrade to Agency, or moving content between two workspaces you already own. # How it works Every workspace is its own isolated row of data: its own episodes, connected accounts, brand voice, and members. Adding a brand space does not "share" one account across brands, it gives your login access to a second, completely separate workspace. Plan limits (episodes, AI generations, clips) are pooled at the account level and explained in Usage limits and monthly allowances ; everything else about a workspace stays private to it. # One workspace per brand Each workspace keeps its content, connected accounts, brand voice, and approval links completely separate from every other workspace. Nothing you set up for one client brand shows up in another, so it is safe to run several client brands from the same Vidflow account. See Workspaces and brand spaces for the full list of what a workspace isolates. # Adding more brand spaces Every account includes one workspace on any plan, including Free. Creating a second workspace requires the Agency plan. Beyond the one workspace Agency includes, owners can purchase additional brand spaces as a metered add-on at $49/month each, adjusted from the Billing page as you need them. title: Workspaces and brand spaces
description: How workspaces isolate one brand's content, accounts, voice, and approvals from every other brand.
headings: Renaming a workspace or changing its details
body: A workspace, also called a brand space, holds one brand's content, clips, and publishing settings. Everything you do in Vidflow happens inside a workspace. Reach for this article when you are setting up a new brand, deciding whether you need a second workspace, or cleaning up one you no longer use. # How it works Every episode, clip, connected account, and setting belongs to exactly one workspace's row in the database. When you switch workspaces, the whole app re-reads its data scoped to that workspace ID, nothing is filtered client-side. That is what makes it safe to run multiple client brands from one login: a workspace member, a connected TikTok account, or a brand sheet created in one workspace is structurally invisible from any other. # What a workspace isolates Each workspace has its own: Episodes, clips, and Content Library. Brand voice, brand sheet, and target audience. Connected social accounts. Schedules, queues, and the Calendar. Approval links. Notification settings. Team members and their roles. Switching to a different workspace switches all of it at once. Nothing from one brand's workspace shows up in another. # Your first workspace Every account includes one workspace, on every plan, at no cost. If you only manage one brand, you likely never need to think about this again.
